title: Search for magnetoacoustic quantum oscillations in the insulating phase of YbB$_{12}$
authors: Ryosuke Kurihara, Atsuhiko Miyata, Koji Araki, Shusaku Imajo, Ruo Hibino, Atsushi Miyake, Sergei Zherlitsyn, Joachim Wosnitza, Hiroshi Yaguchi, Fumitoshi Iga, Masashi Tokunaga, Yasuhiro H. Matsuda
abstract: A highly exotic phenomenon in solid-state physics is the observation of magnetic quantum oscillations in insulators. For instance, in the Kondo insulator YbB$_{12}$ various groups reported the observation of such oscillations seemingly originating from Fermi surfaces, though this contradicts the concept of an insulator having no charged quasiparticles. In this study, we searched for quantum oscillations in YbB$_{12}$ by using bulk-sensitive ultrasonic experiments in high magnetic fields up to 65 T and down to 485 mK. For that, we utilized an YbB$_{12}$ single crystal that, in previous experiments, revealed oscillations in the magnetoresistance in the insulating state. We confirmed oscillation-like behavior of the magnetoresistance as well as field-dependent oscillations in the magnetocaloric effect. However, we could not observe magnetoacoustic quantum oscillations in the insulating state, only in the field-induced metallic state. In the insulating state, we found some anomalies in our ultrasound data, the origin of which remains elusive. Our findings provide further information on the puzzling behavior of the insulating state of YbB$_{12}$.
summary: This study uses ultrasonic measurements to search for magnetoacoustic quantum oscillations in the Kondo insulator YbB12. While oscillations were clearly seen in the field-induced metallic state, they were absent in the insulating state, providing critical constraints on the nature of potential neutral quasiparticles in this system.
main_problem: Investigating the origin of magnetic quantum oscillations in the insulating phase of the Kondo insulator YbB12 and determining if they arise from neutral quasiparticles or other exotic mechanisms.
main_result: Magnetoacoustic quantum oscillations were not observed in the insulating state, appearing only in the field-induced metallic phase, though some unexplained anomalies in elastic constants were detected in the insulating phase.
method: Bulk-sensitive ultrasonic pulse-echo measurements in high magnetic fields (up to 65 T) and low temperatures (down to 485 mK), complemented by magnetoresistance and magnetocaloric effect measurements.
model_or_system: The physical system is the Kondo insulator YbB12, characterized by a hybridization between 5d and 4f electrons, featuring an insulating phase at low fields and a field-induced metallic phase above approximately 45 T.
key_observables: Elastic constants (C11 and C44), ultrasonic velocities (v11 and v44), magnetoresistance (MR), and magnetocaloric effect (MCE).
important_parameters: Magnetic fields up to 65 T, temperatures down to 485 mK, cyclotron mass m* approx 15me, and oscillation frequency approx 700 T.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the applicability of the Lifshitz-Kosevich formula to describe oscillatory contributions to the elastic constant.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the crystal structure and a phase diagram; Figure 4 illustrates the estimated upper bounds of the deformation potential and interaction strength.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of oscillations in insulators, describes the experimental setup and YbB12 sample, presents results for MR, MCE, and ultrasonic velocities, discusses the absence of oscillations in the insulating phase versus the metallic phase, and concludes with an analysis of the observed elastic anomalies.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Crystal structure of YbB12 produced using vesta [31]. (b) Sketch of the phase diagram of YbB12. At low temperatures, besides the observation of magnetic quantum oscillations (MQOs) in the field-induced metallic phase, MQOs have been reported to appear in the insulating phase just below the insulator-metal (IM) transition field HIM. (c) Sketch of the electron-phonon interaction leading to magnetoacoustic quantum oscillations. Particles at the Fermi energy (EF) interact with low-energy acoustic phonons with small wavevectors. (d) Schematic illustration of the modulation of a Fermi surface (FS) and its extremal cross-sectional area SF, taken perpendicular to the magnetic field µ0H,...

FIG. 2. (a) Magnetic-field dependence of the resistivity, ρxx, of YbB12 for I||H||[001] at 560 mK during the up sweep of the magnetic field. The inset shows the data over the full field range with the red (black) line indicating the up (down) sweep. The arrow indicates the IM transition at about 45 T. (b) Magnetic-field dependence of the sample temperature during the down sweep of the magnetic field aligned along [001]. The open circles show the raw data, and the solid line indicates 10-point binomial-smoothed data. The down arrows indicate the fields at 36.5 and 38.9 T, where local minima appear. The inset shows data down to zero field. The up arrows indicate local minima at 19.6 and 30 T.

FIG. 3. Magnetic-field dependence of the relative change of the longitudinal elastic constant ∆C11/C11 = [C11(H) −C11(H = 0)]/C11(H = 0) at several temperatures for H||[001] up to about (a) 65 T and (b) 44 T in the range of 32–43 T. Here, the ultrasound wavevector q and the polarization ξ are parallel to [001]. (c) Relative change of the transverse mode (q||[001], ξ||[100]) with the elastic constant ∆C44/C44 = [C44(H) −C44(H = 0)]/C44(H = 0) at several temperatures for H||[001]. The arrows in panel (a) show weak anomalies around 10 and 30 T. The data at 485 mK and 1.4 K were measured at the ISSP, and the others at HLD. The colored (gray) symbols indicate data taken during the up (down)...
